claim_text: MRI embeddings predict AD vs CN
head: linear probe

site_scanner.detail: outcome exceeds scanner and the margin CI excludes zero (margin +0.25 [95% CI +0.16, +0.33])

brain_age.detail: brain-age R2=0.57, MAE=6.4yr; effect retained 94% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 1% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control

biomarker_anchor.detail: no plasma p-tau217 / GFAP coverage at usable n — cannot anchor (route to ADNI/EPAD)

biology_hypothesis: Survivor “MRI embeddings predict AD vs CN” routes to a amyloid-cascade (tau-driven) mechanism, keyed on p-tau217 and amyloid positivity. Plasma p-tau217: among the strongest blood AD biomarkers. AD-vs-CU AUC ~0.93-0.98; MCI-vs-CU ~0.94. Correlation with a structural probe score is MODEST (r~0.3-0.55), not redundant. SUPPORTED. The structural probe is read as a downstream imaging correlate of that molecular process, not an independent discovery.
next_experiment: Target N: ~120 complete-case subjects with the routing biomarker.
Direction: the structural probe score should track plasma p-tau217 (expected r~0.3-0.55, modest not redundant) and be enriched in amyloid-positive subjects.
Kill criterion: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
falsification: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
If the probe score's biomarker correlation disappears after residualizing against a scanner-predicting direction, the survivor was leakage after all.
If a held-out cohort fails to reproduce the effect (drop below the promotion floor), the mechanism claim is withdrawn.
caveats: Corroborated by REAL cross-cohort replication (held-out cohort), not a molecular marker: no open dataset pairs MRI with plasma p-tau217/GFAP. Replication is genuine independent evidence but weaker than a molecular anchor — a plasma-anchored confirmation needs gated data.
Completeness: 1/5 gauntlet tests could not run; score is renormalized over the tests that did.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.
A ‘strong candidate’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.57, MAE=6.3yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
There is no usable plasma p-tau217 / GFAP anchor here, so the molecular gate is unmet — the finding rests on imaging alone.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

evidence_ledger.detail: outcome exceeds scanner and the margin CI excludes zero (margin +0.25 [95% CI +0.16, +0.33])
brain-age R2=0.57, MAE=6.4yr; effect retained 94% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 1% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control
no plasma p-tau217 / GFAP coverage at usable n — cannot anchor (route to ADNI/EPAD)

narration: The claim “MRI embeddings predict AD vs CN” lands at “strong candidate” (100/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.82 separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0)) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It cleared the gauntlet's promotion floor: the effect exceeds the scanner-prediction floor on subject-disjoint splits and is worth a confirmatory follow-up — as a hypothesis, not a settled result. Where it stops being true: that a molecular anchor exists at all — with no p-tau217 / GFAP correlation measured here, the finding rests on imaging alone and would not survive a demand for orthogonal pathology. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Discovery Engine only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

adjudication.prosecution: The claim that OASIS structural-derived features (weight-free feeder; nWBV/eTIV/ASF) separate AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) does not survive scrutiny. Even granting a +0.25 AUC leakage margin, that headroom is thin enough that acquisition confounds could account for much of the apparent effect. No single dimension cleanly collapses, but absence of a knock-out is not the same as demonstrated biology. The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
adjudication.defense: The signal separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Site / scanner leakage, Brain-age control, Replication split. The outcome exceeds the scanner-prediction floor by +0.25 AUC on subject-disjoint splits — the effect is not merely which machine acquired the scan. The molecular anchor is incomplete, so the defense rests on the imaging survivors alone and asks only for follow-up, not acceptance.
adjudication.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 100/100 places this finding at 'strong candidate'. 1 dimension(s) could not be run and were excluded from the denominator — the verdict reflects only the evidence actually gathered. The +0.25 AUC leakage margin clears the acquisition confound, which is why the defense retains standing. The finding provisionally meets the promotion floor and may proceed to the biomarker-gated biology step — as a hypothesis to test, not a conclusion to bank.

critique: A ‘strong candidate’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.57, MAE=6.3yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
There is no usable plasma p-tau217 / GFAP anchor here, so the molecular gate is unmet — the finding rests on imaging alone.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.
revised_caveats: Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.

Plasma p-tau217: among the strongest blood AD biomarkers. AD-vs-CU AUC ~0.93-0.98; MCI-vs-CU ~0.94. Correlation with a structural probe score is MODEST (r~0.3-0.55), not redundant. SUPPORTED. The structural probe is read as a downstream imaging correlate of that molecular process, not an independent discovery.
